metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (Workflow tool, claude 2.1.165); daemon_capability: full (parent-link + journal-drop fix #565 on this branch); driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready
route: record (build prerequisite: daemon from branch fix/565-workflow-subagents)

## Reasoning

### agent (yes)

Claude Code ships a first-class Workflow tool that fans out N parallel agents on explicit opt-in ("run a workflow ..."). The installed binary (claude 2.1.165 at ~/.local/bin/claude) carries the literal subagent definition `{agentType:"workflow-subagent",whenToUse:"Internal subagent for workflow script orchestration.",tools:["*"],...}` plus the plugin-workflow loader (loadPluginWorkflows). Verified live on this machine: run wf_854deede-0ff in parent session ee963f7d fanned out three agents, each running a trivial task + sleep, completing in ~30s.

On-disk layout (verified live): agents write transcripts one level deeper than plain Task subagents — `<parent-session-dir>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl` — alongside `agent-*.meta.json` ({"agentType":"workflow-subagent"}) and a `journal.jsonl` replay journal; a persisted script lands at `<parent>/workflows/scripts/<name>-<run-id>.js`.

### daemon (full)

Observation is via the standard claudecode fswatcher path (FilesUnderRoot over ~/.claude/projects/, agent.go:46) — no new daemon capability. Two shape gaps the pre-fix detector had are closed on this branch (commit 76f9a576, PR #635):

1. Parent linking. deriveParentSessionID previously only matched `.../subagents/<agent>.jsonl`, so workflow agents (one level deeper) surfaced as top-level sessions with ParentSessionID unset (parent children:0 throughout the run). The fix adds workflowRunRoot, matching `.../<parent>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl` and returning the parent session dir (helpers.go). Once ParentSessionID is bound, the existing child lifecycle applies unchanged: KindParentLinked emission (session_detector_activity.go), SubagentCount derived from live linked children, the liveness sweep, and badge re-evaluation.

2. Journal drop. journal.jsonl is a `.jsonl` file, so the fswatcher emits EventNewSession for it (watcher.go:214 filters to .jsonl only); pre-fix it became a session named "journal" in project "unknown". The fix's isWorkflowBookkeepingFile (helpers.go) returns true for any non-`agent-*` .jsonl inside a run dir, and handleTranscriptEvent drops it before any lifecycle recording (session_detector_activity.go). The other bookkeeping files (`agent-*.meta.json`, the persisted `.js`) are never candidates — the fswatcher only tails `.jsonl`.

Mapped to the user-observable acceptance criteria:
- 'Parent ready → working when workflow starts' — parent's outstanding Workflow tool_use blocks IsAgentDone (same mechanism as the Task tool in foreground-subagent).
- 'Each child ParentSessionID = parent.session_id, a subagent not a top-level session' — workflowRunRoot derivation + KindParentLinked.
- 'SubagentCount(parent) = N while N children alive' — derived from live linked children; tested by TestSessionDetector_WorkflowAgent_LinkedToParent.
- 'No session named journal, no unknown-project session' — isWorkflowBookkeepingFile drop; tested by TestSessionDetector_WorkflowJournal_NotASession.
- 'Children working → ready as they finish; SubagentCount decreases' — ordinary child lifecycle once linked.
- 'Parent working → ready only after last child ends' — pending Workflow tool_use until the run reports back.
- 'After sweep SubagentCount(parent) returns to 0' — the standard child liveness sweep.

### driver (ready)

The recipe is a single opt-in prompt ("run a workflow ...") followed by wait_turn and a settle sleep. driver-interactive.sh implements send, wait_turn, and sleep, so no step is missing. The verified-live recipe runs the workflow as a background task with each agent doing a trivial task + `sleep 20` so the observer catches working states.

## Provisionality

daemon=full is grounded in the fix's unit tests (path derivation + journal drop) and the verified live on-disk layout, but the live recording on this machine was captured against a PRE-FIX daemon (reference only, NOT replaydata) and therefore shows the bug. A meaningful recording REQUIRES a daemon built from this branch; confidence is 0.85 (not higher) until `record` produces a post-fix recording that promotes the lifecycle/sweep timing from provisional to settled.
caveats: The live recording at ~/.local/share/irrlicht/recordings/2026-06-05T153734-186c4f.jsonl was captured against a PRE-FIX daemon and shows the bug (agents as top-level sessions, a 'journal' session in project 'unknown'). It is reference-only and must NOT be promoted into replaydata.

PID discovery resolves the single shared claude PID via CWD matching ('discovered pid ... for claude-code session', verified live); the workflow agents share the parent process, so they have no distinct PID — liveness is driven by transcript activity + the sweep, exactly like plain Task subagents.

The Workflow tool requires explicit opt-in wording in the prompt ('use a workflow' / 'run a workflow'); a generic 'do these 3 things in parallel' prompt elicits plain Task subagents (scenario 3-1), not the deeper workflows/<run-id> layout this cell asserts.

sources.note: deriveParentSessionID + workflowRunRoot match .../<parent>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl and return the parent session dir; isWorkflowBookkeepingFile flags non-agent-* .jsonl in a run dir (journal.jsonl). Fix commit 76f9a576.
handleTranscriptEvent drops isWorkflowBookkeepingFile paths before any lifecycle recording; KindParentLinked is emitted once a child's ParentSessionID binds.
TestSessionDetector_WorkflowAgent_LinkedToParent (agent-a1 links to parent-wf) and TestSessionDetector_WorkflowJournal_NotASession (journal.jsonl creates no session, records no lifecycle events).

watcher.go:214 — EventNewSession fires only for .jsonl files, so agent-*.meta.json and the persisted workflows/scripts/<name>.js are never candidate sessions; only journal.jsonl needs the explicit drop.
strings | grep workflow surfaces {agentType:"workflow-subagent",...} + loadPluginWorkflows in claude 2.1.165 — the Workflow tool shipped.
Live run dir layout: agent-*.jsonl + agent-*.meta.json ({"agentType":"workflow-subagent"}) + journal.jsonl; persisted script under <parent>/workflows/scripts/. Verified 2026-06-05.
Issue #565 — pre-fix bug (workflow agents top-level + 'journal'/'unknown' phantom session); live repro run wf_854deede-0ff, session ee963f7d, 2026-06-05.

preconditions: claude CLI >= 2.1.x on PATH and authenticated; the Workflow tool must be available (verify: strings $(which claude) | grep workflow-subagent)
irrlichd RECORDING and BUILT FROM branch fix/565-workflow-subagents (commit 76f9a576 / PR #635) — a pre-fix daemon would capture the bug
claude session can run a workflow (Workflow tool exposed for the default model)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude's trust dialog appears once and the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Run a workflow that fans out 3 parallel agents. Each agent should do one trivial task and then run `sleep 20` via Bash so the work takes about 20 seconds. Run the workflow as a background task and reply 'workflow started' once it is launched.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent UUID enter ready, then a ready→working transition driven by the user prompt
events.jsonl contains >= 1 parent_linked event whose child session lives under <parent>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl
while the workflow runs, SubagentCount(parent) reflects the live child count (peaks at ~3) and the children appear as subagents of the parent — never as top-level sessions
NO session is named 'journal' and NO session is attributed to an 'unknown' project (the run's journal.jsonl is dropped)
each workflow child transitions working → ready as it finishes; SubagentCount decreases as each ends
the parent transitions working → ready only after the last child ends
after the children are swept, SubagentCount(parent) returns to 0
final session state for the parent is ready
